title: Door Lock Error
shortDescription: The washer door lock mechanism has failed to engage properly.
fullDescription: The E1 error code on Hisense washers indicates the door lock assembly cannot secure the door within the expected time. The control board sends a signal to the door lock solenoid, and if confirmation is not received within approximately 20 seconds, the E1 code is triggered and the wash cycle will not start.

causes.cause: Wiring harness fault between lock and control board

symptoms: Washer will not start and displays E1
Door appears closed but lock indicator is off
Clicking sound from the door lock area without engaging
Door remains locked after cycle and power off
Intermittent E1 that clears after reopening the door

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and inspect the seal and latch area for clothing, debris, or objects preventing full closure. Clean the latch strike area.

fixSteps.title: Verify door alignment
fixSteps.instruction: Close the door slowly and observe the latch engagement. If misaligned, check the hinge screws and tighten as needed.

fixSteps.title: Test the door lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer. Access the door lock behind the front panel. Disconnect the wire connector and test continuity with a multimeter. No continuity indicates a failed lock.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door lock
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the old lock assembly and install the replacement. Reconnect wiring and test.

whenToCallPro: If the door lock needs replacement and you are not comfortable removing panels, or if the control board is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense washer E1 error door lock fix

faq.q: How do I unlock the door when E1 is displayed?
faq.a: Unplug the washer for 3 minutes. If the door remains locked, use the emergency release tab behind the drain filter access panel.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause E1?
faq.a: A power surge can disrupt the lock control signal. Try a power reset first before investigating hardware.

faq.q: Is E1 a common error on Hisense washers?
faq.a: E1 is one of the more frequently reported errors. It often results from simple door alignment or obstruction issues.

title: Water Drain Error
shortDescription: The washer cannot drain water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: The E2 error code on Hisense washers indicates the machine failed to drain water from the drum within the allotted time, usually 8 minutes. This can be caused by a clogged drain filter, blocked drain hose, or a failed drain pump motor.

causes.cause: Foreign object in drain pump impeller

symptoms: Standing water in the drum after cycle
Buzzing from the drain pump area

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the filter access panel at the bottom front. Place towels and a pan below. Slowly unscrew the filter to drain water. Remove and clean the filter thoroughly.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the drain hose for kinks or blockages. Ensure it is not inserted more than 15 cm into the standpipe.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With the filter removed, use a flashlight to check for objects blocking the impeller. Spin it by hand to verify free rotation.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer and disconnect the pump motor. Test with a multimeter for 5 to 20 ohms resistance. Replace if no continuity.

whenToCallPro: If the drain pump motor has failed or plumbing is blocked.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense washer E2 error drain fix

faq.q: How do I drain water manually?
faq.a: Use the drain filter at the bottom front. Place towels and a pan, then slowly open the filter to let water drain out.

faq.q: Can too much detergent cause E2?
faq.a: Yes. Excess suds can impede drainage. Always use HE detergent in recommended amounts.

faq.q: How often should I clean the drain filter?
faq.a: Clean the drain filter monthly, or more often if you wash heavily soiled items or pet bedding.

title: Water Fill Error
shortDescription: The washer did not fill with water within the expected time.
fullDescription: The E3 error code indicates the Hisense washer did not detect the required water level within approximately 10 minutes. Causes include closed supply valves, clogged inlet screens, or a faulty water inlet valve.
symptoms: Washer does not fill with water
Very slow fill that times out
Only hot or cold water enters

fixSteps.title: Check water supply valves
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure both hot and cold supply valves are fully open.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet screens
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water, disconnect hoses, remove and clean the mesh filter screens.

fixSteps.title: Check hoses
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect for kinks or damage. Replace if needed.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug washer, disconnect valve wiring, test solenoid resistance (500-1500 ohms normal).

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve is faulty or water pressure is consistently low.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense washer E3 error water fill fix

faq.q: Can I reset E3?
faq.a: Unplug for 1 minute and replug. The error clears if the water issue is resolved.

faq.q: Why does E3 happen only on hot cycles?
faq.a: The hot water valve may be clogged while the cold valve works fine. Check both inlet screens.

faq.q: What pressure does the washer need?
faq.a: Minimum 20 PSI water pressure is typically required for proper fill operation.

title: Water Overflow Error
shortDescription: Water level has exceeded the safe maximum.
fullDescription: The E4 error code indicates the water level in the drum has risen above the maximum safe threshold, triggering the overflow protection. A stuck-open inlet valve or faulty pressure sensor is typically responsible.
symptoms: Water overflows from door seal or tray
Washer continues filling past normal level
Water visible above normal drum level

fixSteps.title: Shut off water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Immediately turn off water supply valves to stop water flow.

fixSteps.title: Check pressure switch hose
fixSteps.instruction: Remove top panel and check the pressure switch hose for kinks, cracks, or disconnection. Blow through it to verify it is clear.

fixSteps.title: Test the pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and blow into the switch port. Listen for a click. If no click, replace the switch.

fixSteps.title: Check the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With the washer unplugged, check if water still drips through the inlet valve. A stuck-open valve must be replaced.

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve is stuck open, which poses a flooding risk.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense washer E4 error overflow fix

faq.q: Is E4 a flood risk?
faq.a: Yes. Turn off water supply immediately and do not use the washer until repaired.

faq.q: Can a clogged pressure hose cause E4?
faq.a: Yes. The control board cannot detect water level, causing continued filling.

faq.q: How do I test if the inlet valve is stuck?
faq.a: With the washer unplugged, if water still flows through the valve, it is stuck open and must be replaced.

title: Motor Error
shortDescription: The washer motor has failed to start or run at the correct speed.
fullDescription: The E5 error code indicates the drive motor is not functioning properly. The motor may not start, may stall during operation, or may run at incorrect speeds. Causes include overloading, worn motor components, or a faulty motor control board.
symptoms: Drum does not spin or agitate
E5 during wash or spin cycle
Motor hums but drum does not move

fixSteps.title: Reduce the load
fixSteps.instruction: Remove items to reduce load size. Redistribute remaining items evenly.

fixSteps.title: Check for jammed objects
fixSteps.instruction: Rotate the drum by hand. Listen for scraping. Remove any objects stuck between drum and tub.

fixSteps.title: Inspect motor brushes
fixSteps.instruction: Access the motor and remove carbon brush holders. Brushes should be at least 1 cm long. Replace both as a pair if worn.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect motor wiring and test windings with a multimeter. Also test the hall sensor. Replace faulty components.

whenToCallPro: If motor or inverter replacement is needed.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense washer E5 error motor fix

faq.q: Can overloading damage the motor?
faq.a: Repeated overloading accelerates motor wear. Follow the weight limits in your user manual.

faq.q: How do I know if brushes are worn?
faq.a: Remove the brush holders and measure. Brushes shorter than 1 cm need replacement.

faq.q: Is a burning smell dangerous?
faq.a: Yes. Unplug immediately. A burning smell indicates overheating or severely worn brushes.

title: Heating Error
shortDescription: The water heater has not reached the target temperature.
fullDescription: The E6 error code indicates the washer heater element failed to heat water to the set temperature within the expected time. The heater may be faulty, the temperature sensor may be misreading, or wiring may be damaged.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer. Access the heater at the bottom of the tub. Disconnect and test resistance (20-40 ohms normal). Test for ground fault.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and test resistance. At room temperature expect 20K-50K ohms.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect all connections between heater, sensor, and control board. Reseat all connectors.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the heater element or NTC sensor as identified. Ensure the seal is properly seated.

whenToCallPro: If not comfortable working with electrical components near water.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense washer E6 error heater fix

faq.q: Can I use cold wash only?
faq.a: Cold wash cycles may work, but the heater should be repaired for full functionality.

faq.q: Is the heater dangerous if grounded?
faq.a: Yes. A ground fault can trip breakers and pose shock risk. Replace immediately.

faq.q: How long should heating take?
faq.a: Typically 15-30 minutes depending on target temperature and initial water temperature.

title: Motor Speed Sensor Error
shortDescription: The motor speed sensor is not returning correct signals.
fullDescription: The E7 error code indicates the control board cannot read motor speed accurately. The hall sensor or tachometer on the motor is malfunctioning, preventing proper speed control for wash and spin cycles.

causes.cause: Loose wiring between motor and board

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 2 minutes and restart. A temporary glitch may clear.

fixSteps.title: Check motor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect all connectors between motor and control board. Reseat loose connections.

fixSteps.title: Test hall sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and test resistance. Compare with service manual specs.

fixSteps.title: Inspect motor rotor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove rotor and check magnets for cracks or displacement. Replace if damaged.

whenToCallPro: If rotor replacement is needed or control board is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense washer E7 error motor speed sensor fix

faq.q: Is the hall sensor separate from the motor?
faq.a: Usually yes. It is a small sensor mounted on the motor stator and can be replaced individually.

faq.q: Can overloading cause E7?
faq.a: A severely overloaded drum can stall the motor, causing erratic sensor readings.

faq.q: Do I need the full motor for E7?
faq.a: Not usually. Replace the hall sensor first. Only replace the full motor if windings or rotor are damaged.

title: Water Level Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The water level pressure sensor is returning abnormal readings.
fullDescription: The E8 error code means the water level sensor is stuck or returning values outside the expected range. This prevents the washer from properly managing fill and drain operations.
symptoms: Cycle does not progress past fill stage
Incorrect water level for selected cycle

fixSteps.title: Check pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Remove top panel. Verify the rubber hose is connected and not kinked or cracked.

fixSteps.title: Clear blockages
fixSteps.instruction: Blow through the hose to verify it is clear. Check the air dome fitting on the tub.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Blow into the sensor port and listen for a click. Test with multimeter if no click.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove old sensor and install replacement. Reconnect hose and wiring.

whenToCallPro: If sensor and hose are fine but E8 persists.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense washer E8 error water level sensor fix

faq.q: Can a blocked hose cause overflow?
faq.a: Yes. Without water level feedback, the washer may overfill.

faq.q: Is the sensor expensive?
faq.a: No. The sensor costs $10 to $25 and is easy to replace.

faq.q: How do I know if the hose is blocked?
faq.a: Blow through it gently. You should feel no resistance.

title: Overflow Protection Triggered
shortDescription: The base flood sensor has detected water in the bottom of the washer.
fullDescription: The E9 error code is triggered when the anti-flood sensor detects water accumulation in the base pan. This safety feature stops all water operations to prevent flooding. An internal leak is the most likely cause.

fixSteps.title: Check for visible leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water supply and unplug. Inspect under and around the washer for water.

fixSteps.title: Inspect door seal
fixSteps.instruction: Check the rubber gasket for tears, cuts, or debris preventing a proper seal.

fixSteps.title: Check internal hoses
fixSteps.instruction: Remove bottom or rear panel. Tighten all hose clamps and check for cracks.

fixSteps.title: Dry the base pan
fixSteps.instruction: Tilt the washer to access the base. Use towels to dry all water. Verify the float sensor drops back to resting position.

whenToCallPro: If the outer tub is cracked or leak source cannot be identified.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense washer E9 error leak flood fix

faq.q: Can I reset E9?
faq.a: You can clear it by unplugging and drying the base, but it returns if the leak is not fixed.

faq.q: Is E9 an emergency?
faq.a: Address it promptly to prevent water damage.

faq.q: Why does E9 appear during spin?
faq.a: Centrifugal force can push water through small gaps in the door seal or loose connections.

title: Communication Error
shortDescription: The main control board and display board cannot communicate.
fullDescription: The E10 error code indicates a communication failure between the main control board and the display board. Loose ribbon cables, corroded connectors, or failed boards can cause this error.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes to discharge. Plug back in.

fixSteps.title: Reseat ribbon cable
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug, remove top panel, disconnect and reseat the cable between display and main board.

fixSteps.title: Clean connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Check for corrosion. Clean pins with isopropyl alcohol.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty board
fixSteps.instruction: If reseating fails, replace the display board first, then main board if needed.

whenToCallPro: If the issue persists after reseating connectors.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense washer E10 error communication board fix

faq.q: Can a power surge cause E10?
faq.a: Yes. Surges can damage communication circuits. Use a surge protector.

faq.q: Should I replace display or main board first?
faq.a: Start with the less expensive display board.

faq.q: Why is E10 intermittent?
faq.a: Intermittent E10 usually indicates a loose connection rather than failed board.

title: Unbalanced Load Error
shortDescription: The washer has detected an unbalanced load during spin.
fullDescription: The E11 error code indicates the washer detected excessive vibration from an unbalanced load during the spin cycle. Multiple redistribution attempts have failed.

fixSteps.title: Redistribute laundry
fixSteps.instruction: Open door and spread items evenly. Break up tangled items.

fixSteps.title: Level the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Use a bubble level. Adjust leveling feet until perfectly level in both directions.

fixSteps.title: Check shipping bolts
fixSteps.instruction: On new installations, verify all transit bolts are removed from the rear.

fixSteps.title: Inspect shock absorbers
fixSteps.instruction: Push down on drum and release. It should settle quickly. Replace if it bounces.

whenToCallPro: If suspension components are worn or drum bearing is damaged.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense washer E11 error unbalanced fix

faq.q: Can a single blanket cause E11?
faq.a: Yes. Add towels to balance the load.

faq.q: Are shipping bolts a common cause?
faq.a: Yes. New installations with shipping bolts left in are a very common cause.

faq.q: Does E11 damage the washer?
faq.a: Occasional E11 is normal. Frequent imbalance accelerates wear on bearings and shock absorbers.

title: Unbalanced Error - Auto-Balance Failed
shortDescription: The auto-balance routine has exhausted all attempts.
fullDescription: The UE error code on Hisense washers indicates the auto-balance system has tried multiple redistribution cycles and still cannot achieve acceptable balance for the spin cycle. Manual intervention is required.

causes.cause: Load too small or too large for proper balance
Items tangled creating a concentrated mass
Washer on uneven or soft flooring

fixSteps.title: Redistribute the load
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door. Untangle and spread items evenly. Add or remove items to achieve one-third to three-quarters full.

fixSteps.title: Add balancing items
fixSteps.instruction: For single heavy items, add 2-3 towels of similar weight.

fixSteps.title: Check washer level
fixSteps.instruction: Use a bubble level. Place washer on a solid, level surface.

fixSteps.title: Inspect suspension
fixSteps.instruction: If UE is frequent regardless of load, check shock absorbers and springs for wear.

whenToCallPro: If UE occurs frequently with all load sizes.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense washer UE error unbalanced fix

faq.q: What is the difference between UE and E11?
faq.a: E11 is the initial imbalance detection. UE means all automatic rebalancing attempts have failed.

faq.q: Can soft flooring cause UE?
faq.a: Yes. Carpet or warped subfloors amplify vibrations. Use a solid, level surface.

faq.q: Does a rubber anti-vibration mat help?
faq.a: Yes. Anti-vibration pads can reduce movement and help prevent balance errors.

title: Drain Pump Overtime Error
shortDescription: The drain pump has exceeded its maximum operation time.
fullDescription: The OE error code indicates the drain pump has been running for longer than its maximum allowed time without successfully draining the tub. This differs from E2 in that OE specifically relates to the pump running but not effectively removing water.

causes.cause: Partially blocked drain hose or filter

symptoms: OE displayed after prolonged pump operation
Pump runs but water drains very slowly
Water level drops slightly then stops

fixSteps.title: Clean drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and thoroughly clean the drain filter. Check for partial blockages.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose height
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the hose height is between 60-90 cm. If too high, the pump cannot overcome the head pressure.

fixSteps.title: Clear air locks
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect drain hose from standpipe, hold at drum level, run drain cycle. Raise back once flowing.

fixSteps.title: Check pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With filter removed, inspect the impeller for wear or damage. Replace pump if impeller is worn.

whenToCallPro: If the pump impeller is damaged and needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Hisense washer OE error drain pump fix

faq.q: What is the difference between E2 and OE?
faq.a: E2 means the pump cannot start or drain is completely blocked. OE means the pump runs but drains too slowly.

faq.q: Can an extension hose cause OE?
faq.a: Yes. Extended hoses increase pumping distance and can exceed the pump capacity.

faq.q: How often should I clean the filter?
faq.a: At least monthly, or more often with heavily soiled loads.
